channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Arsenal sign Batlle from Barcelona
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Arsenal have confirmed the signing of Ona Batlle from Barcelona.

item.title: Hull moves within two of Evian Championship lead
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Charley Hull trimmed Aki Iwai's lead to two shots with an encouraging second round at the Amundi Evian Championship.

item.title: Qing Madi Becomes the New Face of Infinix HOT 70 Pro, Set for First Official Brand Appearance at UNILAG Campus Storm
item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: Infinix has officially announced Nigerian music sensation Qing Madi as the face of its all-new HOT 70 Pro, marking the beginning of an exciting campaign that celebrates creativity, individuality and fearless self-expression.​ The partnership brings together one of Africa’s fastest-rising music

item.title: Nnamdi Kanu’s wife laughs off divorce rumour
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Uchechi Okwu-Kanu, the wife of leader of the Indigenous People of Biafra (IPOB), Nnamdi Kanu, has dismissed reports claiming that she has filed for divorce. The post Nnamdi Kanu’s wife laughs off divorce rumour appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Olympic Champion McKeown Withdraws From Commonwealth Games
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Five-time Olympic backstroke champion Kaylee McKeown has withdrawn from this summer’s Commonwealth Games in Glasgow after being diagnosed with glandular fever. The 24-year-old Australian, who holds the Olympic, world, and Commonwealth titles in both the 100m and 200m backstroke, will also miss

item.title: Access Holdings vests over 200 million shares in 77 employees, Agbede emerges largest beneficiary
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Access Holdings Plc has vested more than 200 million shares in 77 employees across its group, with former Executive Director, Business Support, Bolaji Agbede emerging as the largest beneficiary with an allocation of 15.09 million shares. The post Access Holdings vests over 200 million shares in 77 e

item.title: FG pushes extended policy to tackle plastic waste
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Government has reaffirmed its commitment to tackling plastic pollution by strengthening implementation of the The post FG pushes extended policy to tackle plastic waste appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: NSIB Releases Preliminary Report on Private Jet That Landed on Asaba Road
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Nigerian Safety Investigation Bureau (NSIB) has released a preliminary report into the incident involving a Bombardier Challenger 601-3A business jet that mistakenly landed on a paved roadway under construction near Asaba Airport instead of the designated runway. The incident occurred on June 10

item.title: 'What old timers can do' - the evolution of Belgium's golden generation
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: BBC Sport explores what is behind Belgium's progress to the last eight of the World Cup, and whether they were too easily discounted among the contenders.

item.title: Klopp Reveals Liverpool’s Private Jet Pitch To Sign Mbappe Before PSG Move
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Liverpool boss Jurgen Klopp has revealed how the club laid on a private jet and hosted Mbappe’s family in an attempt to sign the France striker before he joined Paris St-Germain in 2017. Speaking as a pundit at the World Cup, Klopp said Liverpool “went all out” to court the

item.title: Kaduna: Reforms Have Improved Service Delivery — Uba Sani
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Governor Uba Sani has promised that Kaduna State remains committed to deepening reforms that strengthen fiscal resilience and improve service delivery. The Governor also pledged that every financial resource that is entrusted to government will deliver measurable value for the people of Kaduna State

item.title: Transfer: KRC Genk hand Akpan new four-year contract
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian defender Christian Akpan has signed a new four-year contract with Belgian Pro League outfit KRC Genk, DAILY POST reports. The versatile defender will now remain at the Cegeka Arena until 2030. The 21-year-old linked up with Genk in 2023, and progressed through the club’s youth system

item.title: Best performing Money Market Funds in Nigeria as of June 2026
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria's money market fund segment expanded to a net asset value (NAV) of N5.97 trillion as of June 26, 2026, up 2.26% from N5.84 trillion recorded in May. The post Best performing Money Market Funds in Nigeria as of June 2026 appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Abia Police Arrest Four Suspected Criminals, Recover Firearms In Anti-Cultism Operation
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Abia State Police Command has arrested four suspected criminals, including three alleged cultists, in separate operations that led to the recovery of firearms, ammunition and tools allegedly used for vandalising government infrastructure. The post Abia Police Arrest Four Suspected Criminals, Rec

item.title: 'What a wonderful start!' | Fitzpatrick birdies FIVE in a row at Scottish Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Matthew Fitzpatrick birdies five in a row to temporarily give himself the solo lead at the Genesis Scottish Open.

item.title: England Sweating On Guehi Fitness Ahead OF Norway World Cup Quarter-Final
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: England defender Marc Guehi is a major doubt for Saturday’s FIFA World Cup quarter-final against Norway after sustaining a minor hamstring strain during the Three Lions’ dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexico. The Manchester City centre-back will undergo a fitness assessment on Friday after initial fears

item.title: Moment South African tourists were chased out of restaurant in Mozambique [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A video circulating on social media has shown a group of South African tourists being asked to leave a restaurant in Mozambique. Many online users have linked the incident to deadly xenophobic attacks against foreign Africans in living in South Africa. The incident reportedly occurred at a restauran

item.title: Inflation Is a Tailwind for European, Morgan Stanley Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: European equities stand to benefit from higher inflation, according to Morgan Stanley’s Marina Zavolock, who says the market underestimates the region’s exposure to sectors that profit from rising prices. "We have a lot of real asset exposure, we have banks exposure,” Zavolock, the firm’s chief Euro

item.title: Mbappe’s Era: How France’s record breaker is becoming a World Cup immortal
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: From lifting the trophy in Russia to producing one of the greatest individual performances ever seen in a World Cup final and continuing his remarkable exploits in North America, the France forward has made the sport's biggest stage his own The post Mbappe’s Era: How France’s record breaker is

item.title: European Darts Open: MVG, Van Veen, Clayton and Wade in action
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Defending champion Nathan Aspinall will face Mickey Mansell or Michal Smejda at the Elten Safety Shoes European Darts Open, with the draw and schedule for this weekend's event in Leverkusen now confirmed.

item.title: We’ve autonomy to confer Blue Silks rank on Nigerian lawyers – NLS, BOSCON to NBA

item.description: Amid the controversy and legal dispute surrounding the introduction of the Blue Silks as an alternative to the title of Senior Advocate of Nigeria, SAN, for lawyers in Nigeria, the Nigerian Law Society, NLS, and the Body of Senior Counsel of Nigeria, BOSCON, have declared that they have autonomy to

item.description: The National Working Committee of the Social Democratic Party, led by Prof. Sodiq Gombe, has made an important clarification regarding a Federal High Court judgment being paraded by its embattled former acting chairman, Shehu Gabam.  In a statement on Friday by the opposition party&#8

item.title: “El-Rufai Didn’t Hold Political Meeting During Medical Consultation” – Aide
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Muyiwa Adekeye, media aide to Nasir el-Rufai, says the former Kaduna governor did not violate any order of the court during his visit to the National Hospital in Abuja. In a statement issued on Thursday, Adekeye said the claim by the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commissio

item.title: OF POPULATION, HOPES AND ASPIRATIONS
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Nigeria needs a serious conversation on population control With the theme, ‘Realising the hopes and aspirations of young people for their futures,’ the 2026 World Population Day will be marked

item.title: POP: 44 Corps members face sanction in Rivers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Forty-four members of the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) posted to Rivers State, have been punished by the management for different offences committed during the service year. The post POP: 44 Corps members face sanction in Rivers appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: These 22 U.S. stocks are backed by company buybacks and insider cash.

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have foiled multiple coordinated infiltration attempts by suspected ISWAP terrorists in Borno State, forcing the insurgents to retreat after fierce engagements across several locations. The post Troops Repel Coordinated ISWAP Infiltration Attempts In Borno appeared firs

item.title: Fubara arrives Rivers State Assembly for 2026 Appropriation presentation
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Rivers State Governor Siminalayi Fubara has arrived at the State House of Assembly to present the 2026 Appropriation Bill to the House for consideration and approval. This follows the Assembly’s approval, two weeks ago, of the 2026–2028 Medium Term Expenditure Framework, MTEF. The  2026–20

item.title: 2027 Governor Yusuf urges lawyers to uphold rule of law
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Kano State Governor, Abba Kabir Yusuf, has called on lawyers to uphold the rule of law and support a peaceful, free, fair and credible electoral process ahead of the 2027 general elections. The post 2027 Governor Yusuf urges lawyers to uphold rule of law appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Super Eagles Defender Pens New Four-Year Deal With KRC Genk
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigerian defender Christian Akpan has signed a new four-year contract with Belgian Pro League club KRC Genk, Completesports.com reports. The new deal will keep the versatile defender at the Cegeka Arena until 2030, underlining the club’s faith in his long-term potential. Akpan joined Genk in 2

item.title: CVR: INEC warns against PVC delivery scam, fake website
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has disowned an online portal on the ongoing Continuous Voter Registration (CVR) exercise currently circulating on some social media platforms. INEC Deputy Director of Information and Publicity, Wilfred Ifogah, in an interview in Abuja, described

item.description: “In 1986, I wrote and submitted my resignation letter to the hospital.” The post “How Hubert Ogunde changed my life, convinced me to quit nursing” — Iya Rainbow appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Social Democratic Party, (SDP), has said that the Independent National Electoral Commission(INEC), has no legal basis to act on a letter seeking to reinstall Shehu Musa Gabam as National Chairman. Recall that on July 9, 2026, a letter was submitted to INEC by Gabam’s lawyers, following the judgm

item.title: Premier League Return ‘Ideal’ For US Star Balogun – Keller
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Tottenham goalkeeper Kasey Keller has tipped USMNT striker Folarin Balogun to return to the Premier League following his impressive performances at the 2026 World Cup. Balogun has been heavily linked with a move away from AS Monaco, with Chelsea reportedly reaching a verbal agreement with the

item.title: Four trapped, three rescued as building collapses in Kano
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A two-storey building under construction at Sultan Road in Kano collapsed around 9 a.m. on Friday, leaving four people trapped beneath the rubble. ‎ As of the time of filing this report, three victims had been rescued while efforts were ongoing to save the fourth. ‎ ‎Eyewitnesses said construction w

item.title: Breaking: Fubara presents N1.85trn 2026 budget
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: According to Governor Fubara, the proposed budget projects a total revenue of N1.854 trillion, representing a 24.49 per cent increase over the 2025 adjusted budget. The post Breaking: Fubara presents N1.85trn 2026 budget appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: How Ondo female student was forced into sex-for-grades by HOD — NANS
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) has alleged that a female student in a tertiary institution in Ondo State was compelled into a sex-for-grades arrangement by her Head of Department (HOD) after she repeatedly failed a compulsory course required for graduation. NANS President, Akin

item.title: Judge’s Absence Stalls EFCC Arraignment Of Ex-Warri Refinery MD
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The planned arraignment of former Managing Director of the Warri Refining and Petrochemical Company, Jimoh Yisawu, on alleged money laundering charges was stalled on Friday after the Federal High Court in Abuja adjourned the matter until July 20 due to the absence of the trial judge. The case, filed

item.title: Operation handshake in Benue will flush out border criminals – Gov Alia
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Benue State Governor, Hyacinth Alia, has thrown his weight behind the Nigeria Police Force’s newly introduced Operation Handshake Patrol, describing it as a strategic initiative that will strengthen security across border communities and eliminate criminal hideouts. Governor Alia said the init

item.title: France faces third major heatwave since May
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: As another intense heatwave sweeps across France, residents are staying indoors, flocking to canals and rivers, or even leaving their homes in search of cooler places. With temperatures reaching 41.1°C in Lodève and almost the entire country under heat alerts, holidaymakers and locals alike fear the

item.title: Coroner rules inquest needed into Nobby Stiles' death
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: An inquest into his death will be held after a corner finds injury consistent with repeated head traumas.

item.title: Tekno reveals secret ailment that made loved ones label him “lazy”
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He noted hat he had to consciously and intentionally heal from the emotional strain of the stigma attached to him.

item.title: As SK Hynix lands in the U.S., here’s why investors should be wary of the chip sector’s ‘bubblelike’ volatility
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Traders can only hope South Korea–style turbulence won’t be imported to Wall Street.

item.title: SMFG Said to Weigh Strategy for India Assets Including Yes Bank
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group Inc. is evaluating its India strategy as it looks for ways to consolidate its presence in one of the world’s fastest-growing banking markets, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Delta reports record revenue and a profit beat, even as fuel costs surge
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Delta Air Lines’ stock was set to gain despite the company saying that profit fell — but beat expectations — even after the air carrier absorbed the highest quarterly fuel expense in its history.

item.title: Kaduna Reigns Supreme As Kada Stars, Queens Extend Hockey Dominance
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Kaduna State has further cemented its reputation as Nigeria’s undisputed hockey powerhouse after Kada Stars and Kada Queens continued their remarkable dominance on both the national and West African stages. The two teams once again underlined their supremacy at the 2026 National Hockey Super League

item.description: The Nigerian Safety Investigation Bureau (NSIB) has released the preliminary report on its ongoing investigation into the serious incident involving a Bombardier Challenger 601-3A aircraft with nationality and registration marks N989BC, operated by VMC Aero Limited. item.title: As algorithms shape financial inclusion, accountability must keep pace
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Unlike traditional credit decisions, where a loan officer can explain the factors that informed an outcome, AI-driven decisions are often based on proprietary models controlled entirely by the financial institution.

item.description: The Lagos State Government has launched an investigation after a viral video allegedly showed an officer of the Lagos State Environmental Sanitation Corps (LAGESC) receiving a bribe from a lawbreaker. item.description: The Northern States Governors’ Forum has appointed former Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) Gen. Martin Luther Agwai (rtd) and former Defence Minister, Alhaji Mahmud Yayale Ahmed as co-chairmen of the Northern Nigeria Security Trust Fund Board. The inauguration, held at Sir Kashim Ibrahim House, Kaduna S

The army, in a statement posted on X on Friday, stated that the

item.title: Iran ramps up regional pressure after renewed US strikes
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Following fresh US strikes during the funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran stepped up pressure by targeting US allies and threatening American bases across the region in an effort to force Washington back to the negotiating table. According to FRANCE 24's Philip Turle, despite Donald

item.title: EU tells Meta to change Facebook, Instagram’s ‘addictive design’
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The European Union has in recent months stepped up its efforts to force Big Tech to better protect users online, especially children. The post EU tells Meta to change Facebook, Instagram’s ‘addictive design’ appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: BioOrbit CEO on Developing Cancer Treatments in Zero Gravity
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Katie King, cofounder and CEO of BioOrbit, discussed the company's recent milestone in producing protein crystals in space. BioOrbit launched a record-breaking mission to the International Space Station, achieving the largest scale production of protein crystals in a microgravity environment by one

item.title: Atiku gives Tinubu seven days to break silence on status of Federal Audit Service Bill
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has called on President Bola Ahmed Tinubu to, within seven days, comply with the constitutional obligation imposed by Section 58(4) by either assenting to the Federal Audit Service Bill and formally communicating to the National Assembly and the Nigerian people h

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Donald Trump has dismissed the remaining two commissioners of the US Election Assistance Commission (EAC), leaving the federal agency responsible for supporting and safeguarding elections without any serving commissioners just months before the country's 2026 midterm elections. The post Tr

item.title: Stocks are seeing increasingly wild swings post-earnings. Why even good numbers often aren’t enough to impress.

item.description: The second-quarter earnings season is off to a volatile start, offering an early test of whether investors’ high expectations for corporate profits can hold up.

item.description: A new monthly chart built to measure momentum, not mass — spotlighting the artists climbing fastest in the genre. In March 2026, Mavo topped the Afrobeats Power Ranking. item.title: A Month After SpaceX IPO, Tesla’s Stock Keeps Holding Its Ground
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: In the weeks leading up to SpaceX’s monster IPO, many Wall Street pros feared the deal represented an existential threat for Elon Musk’s other giant, Tesla Inc. But after a month in the stock market, the outcome is far from clear.

item.title: Polestar owners left ‘holding the bag’ after EV brand pulls out of the US
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Last month, Polestar shocked the auto industry when it announced that it was pulling out of the US. The EV company's decision came after the federal government denied its authorization to continue selling its cars despite a rule banning vehicles with Chinese-made connected vehicle software. Polestar

item.title: ‘I’ve no idea how they got her number’: My student-loan servicer called a friend after I missed a payment. Is that legal?

item.description: “She said it’s the second message they have left.”

item.title: 1,500 Americans filed for bankruptcy each day last year. They should have done this instead.

item.description: Personal bankruptcy filings surged 47% between 2022 and 2025. Here’s what you should do before deciding to file.

item.title: Kenya: How Ruto is boosting budget funding without major tax hikes
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Faced with revenue shortfalls and resistance to new taxes ahead of the 2027 election, the Kenyan government is shifting its fiscal strategy from broad-based tax hikes towards tougher enforcement, increased user fees and private-sector partnerships.

Europe is trailing the US in efforts to develop a space economy, underscored by SpaceX's mega IPO in June. Josef Aschbacher, Director General of the European Space Agency, spo

DAILY POST reports that DJ Chicken had two days ago in a video online, stated that the president cannot win a re-election in 2027, and threatened to kill Seyi Tinubu i

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: In politics, succession is often the ultimate test of leadership. While some leaders leave office without a clear successor, others become consumed by bitter succession battles that fracture parties, stall governance and diminish their legacies. In Ogun State, Governor Dapo Abiodun appears determine

item.title: Freddie Omany: The French teacher who ended up moving millions of payments
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Long before Freddie Omany found himself helping move millions of mobile money transactions across Africa every day, Omany taught French. He translated documents. He worked in government communications. He chased unpaid invoices for Booking.com, adigital travel platform, across Francophone Africa. No

item.description: Chairman, All Progressives Congress (APC) in Kwara State, Prince Sunday Fagbemi, has said that the emergence of the Speaker, State House of Assembly, Hon. Yakubu Danladi Salihu, as the 2027 governorship candidate is for fairness, equity and inclusive development across the state. Speaking during a s

item.title: China Vanke Says First-Half Loss May Widen to $2.2 Billion
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China Vanke Co. said its first-half loss could reach $2.2 billion due to low gross margins on property development projects and asset impairment charges.

item.dc:creator: ICIR Nigeria
item.description: AS the outbreak of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola virus disease spreads across the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda, a Nigerian health expert said the country’s years-long battle with Lassa fever has significantly improved its ability to detect and contain Ebola outbreak. But the

item.title: Security forces repel bandit attack in Katsina, force suspected terrorists to flee
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Security forces have successfully repelled a suspected bandit attack on Gangaren Makurdi Village in Bakori Local Government Area of Katsina State. The development was contained in a post shared on X by security analyst, Bakatsine on Friday. According to the post, the suspected bandits entered the vi

item.title: Instagram and Facebook will likely require a redesign after EU rules they’re ‘addictive’
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Meta is in breach of the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA), a preliminary investigation has found, over the "addictive" design of Instagram and Facebook. It's likely to be forced to redesign both apps and could face a fine of up to $12 billion. The European Commission said Meta "did not adequately ass

item.title: NSIB report exposes infrastructure blackout at Asaba Airport following jet’s highway landing
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: LAGOS — A newly released preliminary report by the Nigerian Safety Investigation Bureau (NSIB) has exposed several infrastructure failures at read more NSIB report exposes infrastructure blackout at Asaba Airport following jet’s highway landing

item.title: LASEPA Trains Waste Pickers, Youths on Used Cooking Oil Collection to Boost Circular Economy
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Funmi Ogundare  The Lagos State Environmental Protection Agency (LASEPA), in collaboration with Ororo Waste Management, has commenced a two-day capacity-building programme for waste pickers, market women and youths on the

item.title: Abia Police Arrest Cultists, Vandal In Umuahia, Recovers Firearms
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Abia State Police Command has arrested Obioma Nmeribe (male, 22 years), Emmanuel Lekwechi (male, 25 years) and Ikemefulla ThankGod Amadi (male, 25 years), for activities suspected to be cultism. According to the police, the arrest took place at the Ubakala axis and was effected by its operatives of

item.title: A-Z World Cup quiz: Name the best performing team for every letter
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: When it comes to the World Cup it's not all about the numbers - the letters are important too!

item.title: 2027: Kwara APC Crisis Won’t Stop Party Victory – Ex-Aspirant
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: .. Says APC Remains Party To Beat At State, National Levels A former governorship aspirant of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Kwara State, Prof. Wale Sulaiman, has dismissed fears that the grievances trailing the party’s governorship primary could affect its chances in the 2027 general elect

item.title: IPOB Notifies Int’l Community, Public Of Administrative Changes
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Indigenous People of Biafra (IPOB), has drawn the attention of members of the United Nations Security Council to the formal and lawful dissolution of the 3rd Administration of the Directorate of State and the inauguration of the 4th Administration headed by Mazi Chris Nwaọgụ. The separatist orga

item.title: Dangote Cement targets 80MTPA capacity, 20% emission cut by 2030
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Dangote Cement Plc has unveiled ambitious plans to boost its production capacity to 80 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) by read more Dangote Cement targets 80MTPA capacity, 20% emission cut by 2030

item.title: Elon Musk is an Inspiration: The Exploration Company's Huby
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Hélène Huby, Founder and CEO of The Exploration Company, says Elon Musk serves as an inspiration for her work to develop reusable rockets from Europe. The Exploration Company is one of several companies leading efforts to boost Europe's space sovereignty. Huby spoke to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. (S

item.title: Regulators urge women manufacturers to boost compliance for competitiveness
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) and the Standards Organisation of Nigeria (SON) have called read more Regulators urge women manufacturers to boost compliance for competitiveness

item.title: Is Work from Home in Nigeria a Scam?
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Once accountability replaces attendance as the true measure of performance, the question is no longer whether employees are working from home. The only question that matters is whether the work gets done. The post Is Work from Home in Nigeria a Scam? appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: British Govt Hails Gov Yusuf As Kano Emerges W’Africa’s Highest-Ranked On Education Spending
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The British Government has congratulated Kano State Governor, Alhaji Abba Kabir Yusuf, as the State has emerged as the highest-ranked subnational government in West Africa for education spending. The commendation was handed in a post shared by Mr Ian Attfield, Senior Education Adviser at the British
